episodes.title: Inside Epstein’s dark empire in Central and Eastern Europe | TVP World Special Report
episodes.description:
Thousands of pages of newly released documents and dozens of unanswered questions – including why some of those responsible have still not been held accountable.

TVP World reporters Gabriela Fesnak and Natalia Komarow reveal the scale of Jeffrey Epstein’s dark international network in Central and Eastern Europe. His associates recruited young girls and models from Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, and other countries across the region.

The victims were often in difficult economic situations. Epstein was not only responsible for the entire operation, he personally oversaw the selection process and organized the transport of women to cities such as Paris and New York. 

It was a system built on manipulation, promises of a career, and dependency that led to abuse. And at the heart of it all are the women who, after years, are done staying silent.

#epstein #network #europe

episodes.title: The ignored victims: Central and Eastern Europe in the Epstein Files | News in Depth
episodes.description:
Thousands of Epstein files have been made public – but what role did Central Europe play in the network? 

TVP World’s Natalia Komarow and Gabriela Fesnak spent weeks poring through declassified Epstein Files to uncover how young women from Poland, Ukraine, Belarus, and beyond were recruited through the modeling world and funneled into the orbit of Jeffrey Epstein's sex trafficking network. 

“These are not the big names that everybody else is talking about,” Komarow said. “For every big name we see in the paper, there’s probably countless girls.”

What also struck our reporters was what she described as “classic banality of evil,” highlighting the huge numbers of meticulous logistical details that Epstein himself was involved in. 

As Fesnak described the case of one particular girl: “[Epstein] was involved in the smallest details of her travel… even the model of the car that is being arranged.”

Tune in to learn about how big New York City and Paris agencies, which one of the models interviewed by our reporters described as “pimps for rich people,” had helped turn Central and Eastern Europe into a hunting ground for Epstein and his operation, and Warsaw into its hub.

“Epstein & Eastern Europe” will premiere on TVP World on Friday, May 8, at 8.45 p.m. Warsaw Time.

Chapters:
00:00 Mapping the logistics: Warsaw as transit hub
04:45 A modeling agent’s role in the network
06:22 A firsthand account
08:37 Epstein’s micromanagement and grooming tactics
11:17 Modeling industry’s pathologies and exploitation of economically vulnerable women
15:08 Cross-border collaboration and money flows
17:51 A wall of silence

episodes.title: The Epstein Files: What remains concealed | On Air
episodes.description:
The Epstein scandal is back at the center of American politics, with the latest armed attack on Donald Trump linked to the controversy. New document releases, missing files, growing public outrage, and bipartisan demands for accountability are fueling one of the biggest political and institutional crises in the U.S.

In this episode of On Air, host Klara Syrewicz and her team examine the latest Epstein files, allegations surrounding Trump and other powerful figures, the role of Ghislaine Maxwell, failures of the justice system, and the explosion of conspiracy theories surrounding the case – including the bizarre “Palm Beach Pete” social media phenomenon. For more information on Epstein’s Eastern European recruitment network see this report: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xjj16ZSpnFU

episodes.title: Is Donald Trump building a cult of personality in the White House? | Control Room: Brussels
episodes.description:
A president or a monarch? Leader of a superpower – or a capricious ruler handing out favors to suit his mood?
 
For many observers, the presidency of Donald Trump marks a new chapter in American political history – one built around an unusually strong personal following and an unpredictable style of leadership. While his supporters admire this approach, for many world leaders it presents a serious political challenge: how to conduct international politics with an unpredictable partner.
 
In this episode of Control Room: Brussels, host Grzegorz Nawrocki and members of the European Parliament Michał Wawrykiewicz (EPP, Poland), Tomáš Zdechovský (EPP, Czech Republic) and Diego Solier (ECR, Spain) react to the U.S. president's State of the Union address before Congress, discussing what it means for transatlantic relations, European security, and the future of the Western alliance.
 
As the geopolitical landscape shifts, Europe faces difficult choices about defense, unity, and global partnerships.

Chapters:
00:00 – President or Monarch?
MEPs react to Trump's State of the Union address

06:10 – Can Europe Defend Itself?
Security, NATO and the war in Ukraine

13:30 – Europe Is Waking Up
Defence spending and strategic independence

20:00 – The Future of the Transatlantic Alliance
Unity, democracy and global partnerships

episodes.title: Epstein files: Why Russian media is celebrating? | Break the Fake
episodes.description:
RT editor-in-chief Margarita Simonyan reacted publicly to the release of the Epstein files, expressing relief that no obviously Russian surnames appeared on the published list. A narrative quickly took shape: while Western elites are potrayed as entangled in scandal, Russia is presented as standing apart, morally untouched. But what do the files actually say? Join Jonasz Rewiński in a new episode of Break the Fake to find out more.

episodes.title: Epstein’s ties to Russian and Belarusian networks revealed | News In Depth
episodes.description:
TVP World's Europe Editor Marcin Zaborowski says the newly released files show Jeffrey Epstein wasn’t just running a sex-trafficking operation; he may have been tangled with Russian and Belarusian power networks. Zaborowski highlights Epstein’s Russia trips, alleged ties to Vladimir Putin, and use of honey traps to collect kompromat. 

Epstein also bankrolled Karina Shulyak, his Belarusian model-dentist girlfriend, and helped her enter Columbia, raising questions about his network. Ghislaine Maxwell’s intelligence-linked family may have passed on to him an existing espionage operation. Zaborowski also explains the political fallout, from pressure on Donald Trump to potential Hillary Clinton testimony, and why Poland now seeks a national security investigation.

Host: Maciej Mikos

episodes.title: From court records to crisis: The global impact of the Epstein files | Press Talks
episodes.description:
Are the Epstein files exposing corruption – or igniting a global information war?

The new episode of Press Talks examines how the release and interpretation of the Epstein court records have evolved from a legal scandal into an internationally destabilizing crisis. As political careers hang in the balance, the discussion explores how sensitive documents can trigger global fallout.

The program is hosted by Jarosław Włodarczyk, President of Press Club Poland, who brings together a distinguished panel of international journalists and analysts to examine how global events are perceived, framed, and contested far beyond Europe’s borders.

episodes.title: Epstein files expose Central Europe as a “hunting ground” | News in Depth
episodes.description:
Matthew Day says the new Jeffrey Epstein files show Central and Eastern Europe – especially Poland  – served as a recruiting ground, with Warsaw as the operation's travel hub for moving young women abroad.

He explains that Epstein’s team didn’t only use agencies to find women; they also drove through villages looking for girls, often targeting poor or broken homes with promises of money, apartments, or education.

According to witness testimony, this allegedly happened at a shocking scale, sometimes around five underage girls a week in the early 2000s. Day also warns that beyond trafficking, there may be a blackmail angle involving powerful people, which is why governments like Poland are treating this as a national security issue.

His bottom line: there are millions of documents still unseen, key names are redacted, and until everything comes out, real accountability is likely to remain nonexistent.

#epstein #poland #trafficking #warsaw #recruitment #scandal
